items.title: USDA SNAP tracker lists approved state waivers restricting items like soda and candy, with multiple 2026 start dates
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP food-restriction waiver tracker lists many approved state demonstrations that restrict purchases such as soda, candy, energy drinks, and other items. The tracker shows multiple 2026 effective dates, including Texas (Apr. 1, 2026), Florida (Apr. 20, 2026), and Missouri (Oct. 1, 2026), alongside earlier Jan. 1, 2026 starts in several states.

items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits for federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens starting Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS says states must implement a statutory change starting October 1, 2026 that limits when states can claim federal matching funds in Medicaid and CHIP for certain noncitizens. CMS notes the change does not affect federal matching funds for treatment of emergency medical conditions and does not change states’ option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: HUD ends EHV/Stability Voucher documentation flexibilities; PHAs must verify SSNs and eligible immigration status
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-05 removes the prior option that let PHAs admit some Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) and Stability Voucher (SV) families without verifying Social Security numbers and eligible immigration/citizenship documentation up front (with a 180-day follow-up window). HUD says PHAs must comply within 60 days of publication, including verifying eligible immigration status and verifying/collecting SSNs for EHV/SV family members.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: HUD rescinds streamlined eligibility verification flexibilities for Housing Choice Voucher, PBV, and public housing admissions
items.summary: NLIHC reports HUD Notice PIH 2026-04 rescinds the streamlined waiver process (from PIH 2024-17) that allowed certain self-certifications and reduced third-party verification requirements for date of birth, disability status, income, and assets—especially for people experiencing homelessness. NLIHC notes previously approved waivers remain valid until expiration, but PHAs must otherwise use standard verification processes.

items.title: Federal Register: ACF proposes reallotment of FY 2025 LIHEAP funds to FY 2026 award recipients
items.summary: The Federal Register published ACF’s preliminary determination that some Fiscal Year 2025 LIHEAP funds are available for reallocation to states, territories, tribes, and tribal organizations that received FY 2026 direct LIHEAP awards. This can shift additional LIHEAP dollars to jurisdictions based on program rules and unspent FY 2025 balances.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash-value benefit amounts; states must allow at least one non-fresh option by April 2026
items.summary: USDA’s WIC memo lists FY 2026 monthly cash-value benefit amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. USDA also notes states must authorize at least one form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: SSA: 2.8% COLA for 2026; SSI federal payment standard rises to $994 (individual) and $1,491 (couple)
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 COLA fact sheet shows a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026 and lists the SSI federal payment standard as $994/month for an eligible individual and $1,491/month for an eligible couple. The same fact sheet also shows the maximum taxable earnings amount rising to $184,500 in 2026.

items.title: FAFSA federal deadline for 2025–26 aid: submit by June 30, 2026; corrections allowed until Sept. 12, 2026
items.summary: Federal Student Aid lists June 30, 2026 (11:59 p.m. Central time) as the federal deadline to submit the FAFSA for the 2025–26 award year. The same page lists Sept. 12, 2026 (11:59 p.m. CT) as the deadline for corrections or updates.
